insight: Browser DAW architecture: Use native Web Audio nodes (BiquadFilterNode, ConvolverNode, DynamicsCompressorNode) for standard effects instead of reimplementing in AudioWorklet. Reserve AudioWorklet exclusively for custom DSP that native nodes cannot do (PolyBLEP oscillators, custom envelopes, modulation routing). Native nodes are C++ implementations -- faster and more battle-tested than JS equivalents.

DAW audio safety: Always place a non-bypassable hard limiter (DynamicsCompressorNode with threshold=-1dBFS, ratio=20:1, attack=0.001s) as the FINAL node before AudioContext.destination. This prevents hearing/speaker damage from feedback loops or gain spikes. Also implement mandatory DFS cycle detection BEFORE every AudioNode.connect() call, not after.

Real-time browser audio rendering: Bypass React state completely for 60fps UI updates (metering, waveforms, playback cursor). Use SharedArrayBuffer written by audio thread, read by requestAnimationFrame + Canvas/direct DOM mutation via useRef. React re-renders at 60fps will thrash the main thread and cause audio scheduling gaps.

For vitest mocks of browser constructors (AudioContext, etc.), use class syntax directly (vi.stubGlobal('AudioContext', MockAudioContextClass)) instead of vi.fn(() => new Mock()). The vi.fn() wrapper breaks 'new' operator semantics.

App.test.tsx MockAudioContext needs createGain, createOscillator, sampleRate, currentTime, and destination properties for any test that renders components using useTransport hook (which creates Metronome and TransportClock internally)

E4 Media Pool: The TDD cycle works well for pure-logic audio modules (magic-bytes, waveform-peaks, decode-pipeline). In-memory storage implementations are better than async-mocked versions for testing - avoid async methods without await by using Promise.resolve() directly. When adding new context providers, remember to update all existing test files that render components depending on the new context (App.test.tsx, DawShell.test.tsx).

Canvas-based arrangement renderers need mock contexts with MockCtx type aliases instead of vi.mocked() on ctx methods to avoid @typescript-eslint/unbound-method errors. Also React refs cannot be read during render - use useState for values returned from hooks.
